# Ethernal

> Ethernal is an open-source block explorer for EVM-based chains. It supports public and private explorers, transaction tracing, contract verification, token and NFT tracking, and L2 rollup features for OP Stack and Arbitrum Orbit chains.

## Product

- [Homepage](https://tryethernal.com): Overview of Ethernal's block explorer platform
- [Pricing](https://tryethernal.com/pricing): Plans from free (Starter) to Enterprise, for public and private explorers
- [Features](https://tryethernal.com/features): Full feature list including contract verification, transaction tracing, token tracking, and analytics
- [Transaction Tracing](https://tryethernal.com/transaction-tracing): Deep transaction trace visualization with call trees, state diffs, and gas profiling
- [App Chains](https://tryethernal.com/app-chains): Block explorer for application-specific chains

## Developer Integrations

- [Hardhat](https://tryethernal.com/hardhat-block-explorer): Hardhat plugin for automatic contract syncing and local development
- [Anvil](https://tryethernal.com/anvil-block-explorer): Foundry Anvil integration for local chain exploration
- [Ganache](https://tryethernal.com/ganache-block-explorer): Ganache integration for Truffle-based development
- [GitHub Actions](https://tryethernal.com/github-actions): CI/CD integration for automated explorer setup
- [Chainstack](https://tryethernal.com/chainstack): Chainstack managed node integration
- [Kaleido](https://tryethernal.com/kaleido): Kaleido enterprise blockchain integration

## L2 Frameworks

- [OP Stack](https://tryethernal.com/op-stack): Block explorer for OP Stack rollups with L1/L2 bridging, batch tracking, and output verification
- [Arbitrum Orbit](https://tryethernal.com/arbitrum-orbit): Block explorer for Arbitrum Orbit chains with batch and withdrawal tracking

## Supported Chains

- [Ethereum](https://tryethernal.com/chains/ethereum): Mainnet explorer
- [Base](https://tryethernal.com/chains/base): Base L2 explorer
- [Optimism](https://tryethernal.com/chains/optimism): Optimism L2 explorer
- [Arbitrum One](https://tryethernal.com/chains/arbitrum-one): Arbitrum One explorer
- [Blast](https://tryethernal.com/chains/blast): Blast L2 explorer
- [Mode](https://tryethernal.com/chains/mode): Mode Network explorer
- [Zora](https://tryethernal.com/chains/zora): Zora Network explorer
- [Polygon PoS](https://tryethernal.com/chains/polygon-pos): Polygon PoS explorer
- [BNB Chain](https://tryethernal.com/chains/bnb-chain): BNB Chain explorer
- [Sepolia](https://tryethernal.com/chains/sepolia): Sepolia testnet explorer
- [Holesky](https://tryethernal.com/chains/holesky): Holesky testnet explorer
- [Base Sepolia](https://tryethernal.com/chains/base-sepolia): Base Sepolia testnet explorer
- [Arbitrum Sepolia](https://tryethernal.com/chains/arbitrum-sepolia): Arbitrum Sepolia testnet explorer
- [Arbitrum Nova](https://tryethernal.com/chains/arbitrum-nova): Arbitrum Nova explorer
- [Degen Chain](https://tryethernal.com/chains/degen-chain): Degen Chain explorer
- [Xai](https://tryethernal.com/chains/xai): Xai gaming chain explorer
- [Proof of Play Apex](https://tryethernal.com/chains/proof-of-play-apex): Proof of Play Apex explorer
- [Redstone](https://tryethernal.com/chains/redstone): Redstone explorer
- [Lisk](https://tryethernal.com/chains/lisk): Lisk L2 explorer
- [Cyber](https://tryethernal.com/chains/cyber): Cyber explorer
- [Mint](https://tryethernal.com/chains/mint): Mint explorer

## Comparisons

- [Blockscout Alternative](https://tryethernal.com/blockscout-alternative): Side-by-side comparison of Ethernal vs Blockscout — setup, runtime, L2 support, pricing
- [Routescan Alternative](https://tryethernal.com/routescan-alternative): Side-by-side comparison of Ethernal vs Routescan — open source vs closed source, self-hosting, vendor lock-in, pricing
- [Etherscan Alternative](https://tryethernal.com/etherscan-alternative): Ethernal as an open-source Etherscan alternative for custom EVM chains — self-hostable, MIT licensed, published pricing vs Etherscan's closed-source EaaS
- [Build vs Buy Block Explorer](https://tryethernal.com/build-vs-buy-block-explorer): Should you build a custom block explorer or use Ethernal? Compare cost ($150K+ vs $0), timeline (3-6 months vs 5 minutes), and capabilities. MIT licensed, fully customizable.

## Free Tools

- [Calldata Decoder](https://tryethernal.com/tools/calldata-decoder): Free online tool to decode Ethereum transaction calldata into human-readable function calls, or encode function signatures into calldata. Supports auto signature lookup via 4byte.directory and custom ABI input. Runs entirely in your browser.
- [4byte Function Signature Lookup](https://tryethernal.com/tools/4byte-lookup): Free tool to look up Ethereum function signatures by their 4-byte selector, or search signatures by name. Uses 4byte.directory and client-side keccak256 hashing.

## Blog

- [On-Chain Engineering Blog](https://tryethernal.com/blog): Technical articles on EVM internals, EIPs, L2 architecture, and smart contract development

## Documentation

- [API Reference](https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/12141908/2s83zfQ5Tf): Postman collection for Ethernal's REST API
- [Documentation](https://doc.tryethernal.com): Full product documentation
- [GitHub](https://github.com/tryethernal/ethernal): Open-source repository
